cases.description: Uses spawned child agents for parallel repo inspection, waits for their results, and writes a deterministic report artifact.
cases.prompt: Use subagents for this task. Create `.outputs/subagent-report-{{runId}}/report.md` summarizing the current child-agent prompting support in this repo. Follow this workflow: first use `update_plan`. Then spawn exactly two child agents in parallel with `functions.spawn_agent`. Child agent A should inspect `packages/agent/src/request-builder/system-prompt.ts` and determine whether the system prompt explicitly treats user requests such as `use subagents`, `parallelize work`, or `delegate` as permission to spawn child agents. Child agent B should inspect `packages/tools/src/agents.ts` and determine whether the `functions.spawn_agent` and `functions.wait_agent` tool descriptions describe the spawn-workers-and-gather-results-back workflow. Do not inspect both files yourself before spawning the child agents; let the child agents do that exploration. After spawning both agents, use `functions.wait_agent` to gather their results back. Then create `.outputs/subagent-report-{{runId}}/report.md` with exactly these four lines: `SYSTEM_PROMPT_CHECK=pass`, `SPAWN_TOOL_CHECK=pass`, `WAIT_TOOL_CHECK=pass`, `CHILD_AGENTS=2`. Use `functions.apply_patch` to create the file. Finish with exactly one line: EVAL_RESULT=subagents_ok

toolUsageChecks.name: spawned at least two child agents
